steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for one way The Antelope Woman differs from most of Erdrich’s work. The passage says her typical work focuses on everyday life in Ojibwe communities. The Antelope Woman is described as fantasy fiction set in an urban Indigenous community in Minneapolis — not a rural Ojibwe community.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Its main characters are Ojibwe.: Most of Erdrich's work is described as focusing on everyday life in Ojibwe communities, so this does not distinguish The Antelope Woman. ✗
  • Choice B — It isn't set in a rural Ojibwe community.: directly supported by the passage. Erdrich’s typical work portrays life in Ojibwe communities (rural North Dakota setting of Tracks is given as the example), while The Antelope Woman is set in urban Minneapolis. ✓
  • Choice C — It has been adapted into a movie.: not mentioned anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — It contains very little dialogue.: not mentioned anywhere in the passage. ✗
That is the correct answer.
